description: Measures and draws an angle using the protractor instrument. The protractor auto-positions at the vertex, aligns its base to the first arm, and sweeps an arc to the second arm. The result behaves as an angle geometry.
utility: Measure and visualize an angle with protractor animation — result is compatible with angle() output

arguments.name: vertex or L1
arguments.description: Vertex point (three-point form) or first line (two-line form)

arguments.name: armEnd1 or L2
arguments.description: First arm endpoint (three-point form) or second line (two-line form)

arguments.name: armEnd2
arguments.description: Second arm endpoint (only in three-point form)

alternates.description: Measure angle at vertex between two arm endpoints
Measure angle between two intersecting lines

examples.scenario: Measure the angle between two line segments using protractor
examples.expression: G = g2d(at(-0.9, 6.6), 30, range(-3, 8), type(geometry)); L1 = line(G, point(G, 1, 1), point(G, 5, 1), type(segment)); L2 = line(G, point(G, 1, 1), point(G, 3, 5), type(segment)); ang = protractorangle(G, L1, L2, t(6))

examples.scenario: Measure all three angles of an equilateral triangle with protractor
examples.expression: G = g2d(at(5,6), size(20), range(-1, 8), type(geometry)); line_a = rulerline(G, point_A, point_B, t(2)); line_b = rulerline(G, point_A, point_C, t(2)); line_c = rulerline(G, point_C, point_B, t(2)); angle_1 = protractorangle(G, line_a, line_b, c(black)); angle_2 = protractorangle(G, line_b, line_c, c(black)); angle_3 = protractorangle(G, line_c, line_a, c(black))

examples.scenario: Measure angle and label it in an equilateral triangle lesson
examples.expression: G = g2d(at(5,6), size(20), range(-1, 8), type(geometry)); angle_1 = protractorangle(G, line_a, line_b, c(black)); label_ang1 = label(G, at(angle_1), "theta_1=60^o", buff(-1, 1), fs(14), c(black))
